strengths: 19 years of progressive full-stack engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure with demonstrated mastery of multiple languages and domains
Recent AI infrastructure work at re:cinq with MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks shows current engagement with AI technologies
Strong community impact through open-source projects (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial), workshops, and conference talks demonstrating ability to communicate technical vision
concerns: AI/ML expertise is recent (April 2025) and limited to infrastructure/tooling with no demonstrated track record of building AI-powered products or platforms prior to current role
No evidence of director-level strategic leadership: missing examples of identifying future product states, advising enterprise customers on AI adoption, or shaping product vision at scale
Cloud expertise mentioned (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) but no demonstrated strategic platform architecture or large-scale cloud strategy decision-making experience
response.justification: The candidate meets the baseline 15-year experience requirement with strong technical execution across multiple domains and recent exposure to AI infrastructure. However, the role requires director-level strategic leadership in AI/cloud platforms with demonstrated products built with AI. The resume shows only recent AI infrastructure work (MCP, agentic workflows) since April 2025 with no prior ML/AI product track record, and lacks evidence of strategic direction-setting, enterprise customer advisory, or platform vision work that defines principal-level roles. The candidate appears positioned as a talented senior engineer gaining AI exposure rather than a recognized AI strategist.
